@@ -33,7 +33,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-4qom2","title":"[SEV] tmux cmd_queue cap only enforced at pop site; unbounded growth when state != Idle","description":"frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs TmuxDomainState.cmd_queue has CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH=50_000 enforced only inside send_next_command at line 289 via cmd_queue.drain(..excess). send_next_command early-returns at line 286-288 when state != Idle, so the drain-oldest logic never runs if tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse.

Four push sites append to cmd_queue with NO cap check:
- line 196: Event::LayoutChange -> ListAllPanes
- line 223: Event::SessionChanged -> ListCommands
- line 234: Event::WindowAdd -> ListAllWindows
- line 375: split_pane (user split) -> SplitPane

Concrete interleaving: (1) tmux sends a command, enters State::WaitingForResponse; (2) tmux server is slow/hung (real-world scenario: remote tmux on lagging SSH link, tmux server overloaded, Event::Guarded response delayed); (3) GUI user resizes window repeatedly -> stream of Event::LayoutChange -> each push_back at line 196 runs without cap check; (4) send_next_command early-returns because state != Idle. Queue grows unbounded -> memory leak, eventual OOM.

The CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH comment at line 32-33 explicitly advertises 'prevent unbounded memory growth during event storms' as a safety invariant. That invariant is currently only upheld at the pop site, where it can't run under the exact condition (stuck tmux) that event storms exploit.

Fix: extract the cap-enforcement logic into a push_command_capped helper that applies the drain-oldest + warn/error logging at every push site. The helper runs under the cmd_queue lock (just acquired by the caller), so it's atomic with the push and has no additional locking cost.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:17:26.601099Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.160715Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.158111Z","close_reason":"Completed: added push_command_capped() helper and routed all 4 production push sites (LayoutChange/SessionChanged/WindowAdd/split_pane) through it. The hard cap is now enforced at push time, not only at pop time — closing the unbounded-growth window when tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse. cargo check passes clean. Commit 1055cffc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-58uc0","title":"[gap] README wait-for rule-fire claim not matched by crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs","description":"README.md:144 claims `ft robot wait-for` blocks until a specific rule fires; grep shows the command is defined as "Wait for a pattern in pane output" at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:2454-2473 and implemented via `wezterm::compile_wait_matcher` + `PaneWaiter` polling at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:17782-17864.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:08.856774Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:49:14.645632Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:49:14.644794Z","close_reason":"done — README said 'blocks until a specific rule fires' but implementation waits for pattern match in pane output; fixed wording (9f9a7c40)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-5zyqe","title":"[mock] dataflow.rs:980 merged compute nodes downgraded to NodeKind::Source","description":"dataflow.rs:980 in DataflowGraph::merge() sets `kind: NodeKind::Source, // placeholder` for merged compute/debounce nodes because ComputeFn is not Clone. This silently degrades merged compute nodes to inert Source nodes — their computation logic is lost. Production behavior should be: either (a) add a NodeKind::InertCompute variant so callers can detect and re-register the compute fn, (b) make ComputeFn clonable via Arc wrapper, or (c) log a warning when a compute node is downgraded during merge. The doc comment at line 965-968 acknowledges the limitation and tells callers to re-register, but the silent downgrade is error-prone.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:32:39.280862Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:39:50.126666Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:39:50.062995Z","close_reason":"implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3599,"issue_id":"ft-5zyqe","author":"jemanuel","text":"Commit cef1c892 switches dataflow ComputeFn to Arc so merge() can preserve Compute/Debounce node kinds instead of downgrading them to Source. Updated merge docs, rewired merge() to clone node kinds, and added regression tests proving merged compute nodes still recompute and merged debounce nodes still buffer+flush. Local verification: rustfmt + git diff --check passed; UBS found no critical issues in crates/frankenterm-core/src/dataflow.rs. Requested rch run is still spending the window in full-workspace sync with no compile/test result line yet.","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:39:50Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-61kg4","title":"events::EventDeduplicator::check: max_capacity=0 degrades to 1-slot ghost cache (parallel to ft-bx4le)","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:843-891 EventDeduplicator::check with max_capacity=0 does NOT disable deduplication. Instead: the novel-key eviction path at line 875 fires (entries.len()=0 >= 0=true), pop_front on empty insertion_order is a no-op, and the insert at line 881-888 still succeeds. Effect on alternating keys A, B, A: each is reported 'New' (because evict-then-insert toggles which key occupies the single slot), but an immediate repeat of the same key returns Duplicate. This is the same 'zero capacity → 1-slot ghost cache' shape fixed in connector_inbound_bridge ft-bx4le (commit 8fdfd114). Fix: early-return DedupeVerdict::New at the top of check when self.max_capacity == 0, matching ft-bx4le's pattern. No state accumulation, consistent 'no dedup' semantics.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:51.129305Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:51.129305Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-61kg4","title":"events::EventDeduplicator::check: max_capacity=0 degrades to 1-slot ghost cache (parallel to ft-bx4le)","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:843-891 EventDeduplicator::check with max_capacity=0 does NOT disable deduplication. Instead: the novel-key eviction path at line 875 fires (entries.len()=0 >= 0=true), pop_front on empty insertion_order is a no-op, and the insert at line 881-888 still succeeds. Effect on alternating keys A, B, A: each is reported 'New' (because evict-then-insert toggles which key occupies the single slot), but an immediate repeat of the same key returns Duplicate. This is the same 'zero capacity → 1-slot ghost cache' shape fixed in connector_inbound_bridge ft-bx4le (commit 8fdfd114). Fix: early-return DedupeVerdict::New at the top of check when self.max_capacity == 0, matching ft-bx4le's pattern. No state accumulation, consistent 'no dedup' semantics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:51.129305Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:00.572881Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:00.572605Z","close_reason":"Landed in bf854b26 (fix(events): bypass dedup when EventDeduplicator max_capacity=0). Short-circuit DedupeVerdict::New at top of check() when max_capacity == 0, mirroring ft-bx4le's pattern for SignalDeduplicator. Regression test dedup_zero_capacity_bypasses_dedup_ft_61kg4 pins the behavior. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-6269x","title":"[reality-gap] ft snapshot restore Unix-only status is not disclosed in README session-persistence matrix","description":"# Claim (README)

README.md:128 Session Persistence matrix row:

    | Session persistence | Supported with backend prerequisite |
    Snapshots, session inspection, restore, and `ft session doctor`
    are implemented, but live restore still depends on the current
    WezTerm mux interop boundary. \`ft restart\` is currently
    Unix-only. |

Disclosed: `ft restart` Unix-only.
Undisclosed: `ft snapshot restore` has the *same* cfg gate.

# Reality (code)

`crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:27100` (ft restart — disclosed):

    eprintln!("ft restart is currently supported only on Unix platforms.");

`crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:39715` (ft snapshot restore — NOT disclosed):

    eprintln!("ft snapshot restore is currently supported only on Unix platforms.");

Same gate, same failure mode. README line 128 specifically mentions
`ft restart` as the Unix-only surface but omits `ft snapshot restore`,
which is also mentioned in the *same* matrix row ("Snapshots, session
inspection, **restore**, and `ft session doctor` are implemented").

A Windows operator reading the matrix is told "restore is
implemented" (true, on Unix) without the cross-platform caveat.

# Failure shape

Operator on Windows runs `ft snapshot restore ...` → eprintln hits
→ exit 1. Workaround unknown.

# Recommended disposition

Update README.md:128 to say:

    Snapshots and session inspection are implemented cross-platform;
    `ft restart` and `ft snapshot restore` are currently Unix-only.

One-line doc change. Alternative: fix the cfg gate. The session
persistence code under `#[cfg(unix)]` likely depends on real Unix
signals / fork semantics — porting is non-trivial. Docs-first is
the pragmatic path.

# Related

ft-2guux previously flagged G2 "`ft restart` Unix-only not in
support matrix" and was closed 2026-04-21 with a README.md:128
update. That update added disclosure for `ft restart` but missed
`ft snapshot restore` — this bead is the follow-up.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"pane8","owner":"pane3-readme-fix","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:44:55.113196Z","created_by":"reality-check","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:49:57.208440Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:49:57.208098Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","platform","reality-gap"]}
 {"id":"ft-682n6","title":"Migrate ~50 tokio-based async tests to asupersync runtime (ft-22x4r tracked)","description":"# Goal
Convert ~50 tests using #[tokio::test] to work with the asupersync runtime. These tests fail at runtime with: task::spawn called outside of Runtime::block_on context. Originally tracked as ft-22x4r.

# Background
The asupersync migration replaced tokio, but ~50 tests still use #[tokio::test]. Count verified 2026-04-06: 50 in .rs files (excluding .bak). Most are in *_labruntime.rs test files (2 per file across ~25 files) plus 2 in dependency_eradication.rs.

# Design
## Discovery (exact count at implementation time)
1. grep -r '#\[tokio::test\]' crates/ --include='*.rs' | grep -v '.bak' > /tmp/tokio_tests.txt
2. Count and categorize by file pattern:
   - *_labruntime.rs files: ~25 files × 2 tests = ~50 tests (async smoke + basic scenario)
   - dependency_eradication.rs: 2 tests
3. For each, identify tokio primitives used: spawn, sleep, channels, timeout

## Migration strategy
- *_labruntime.rs pattern: These all follow the same template (2 tests per file). Once one is migrated, the rest are mechanical.
  Replace: #[tokio::test] with #[test] + runtime_compat::block_on(async { ... })
  Or: if asupersync provides a test macro, use that
- Replace tokio::spawn -> runtime_compat spawn equivalent
- Replace tokio::time::sleep -> runtime_compat::sleep
- Replace tokio channels -> runtime_compat channels

## Batch approach
Since *_labruntime.rs files follow a uniform pattern, write a migration helper that does the mechanical replacement, then verify each file compiles.

## Structured Logging
- Migration log: file, old_macro, new_macro, primitives_replaced, compile_status

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Zero #[tokio::test] in .rs files (verified by grep, excluding .bak).
2. All ~50 previously-failing tests now pass.
3. No semantic changes — same assertions.
4. Total test count preserved.
5. No new tokio dependencies.
6. Commit message lists affected files.

# Implementation Notes
- runtime_compat.rs provides bridge primitives.
- The *_labruntime.rs naming convention suggests these are lab/smoke tests for async behavior.
- This bead unblocks ft-xbnl0.2 (async cutover epic).
- Coordinate with ft-n2j5w (MCP test fix) if any MCP tests also use tokio::test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:58:21.227738Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:55:15.155157Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T02:55:15.154811Z","close_reason":"Verified: zero actual #[tokio::test] annotations remain in crates/frankenterm-core/. All 50 occurrences found by grep are in comments/docstrings/string literals describing the migration that was already done. The *_labruntime.rs files already use #[test] + RuntimeFixture::current_thread().block_on(). No code changes needed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","migration","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-6mxfs","title":"[mor:security-engineering] ws_dir = root.join('.ft','patterns') is not canonicalized before discovery","description":"# Observation

In `load_packs_from_config`
(crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs:949):

    let ws_dir = root_path.join(".ft").join("patterns");
    let discovered = discover_packs_from_dir(&ws_dir)?;

ft-fbee5 (just closed) fixed the case where a symlink INSIDE
`.ft/patterns/` escapes the sandbox. But the parent path is itself
just a join, no canonicalize. A hostile cloned repo can ship:

    .ft/patterns -> /etc

(a symlink at the directory level.) `ws_dir` canonicalizes to
`/etc`, `discover_packs_from_dir(/etc)` iterates there, and the
per-file sandbox check passes because it canonicalizes with root =
`/etc`. The symlink escape is now one level up.

Similar attack surface for `config.resolved_user_packs_dir()` if
that ever returns a joined path the caller doesn't canonicalize.

# Question

Should `load_packs_from_config` canonicalize `ws_dir` and verify the
result resolves *inside* `root_path.canonicalize()` before calling
`discover_packs_from_dir`? That closes the parent-level symlink
vector without needing to change `discover_packs_from_dir`'s
contract.

Stretch: is there a case for centralizing this as a
`sandbox_resolve_dir(dir, root)` helper and using it everywhere the
pack/workflow/policy loaders join a workspace-relative path? The
pattern is probably not unique to pack loading.

# Trust boundary

The repo is the attacker; the user's home directory is the victim.
Everything a hostile repo can make `read_to_string` see is a
potential data-exfil primitive. Treat repo-relative path
construction as crossing a trust boundary, and canonicalize at the
boundary crossing, not at the leaf.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"question","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:35:09.320199Z","created_by":"mor-review","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:51:59.305474Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:51:59.305207Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 0239fbd6 — added sandbox_resolve_dir helper, routed load_packs_from_config's ws_dir through it with 4 regression tests (missing, inside-root, toplevel-symlink-escape, end-to-end).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mor","sandboxing","security"]}
